sources_exist.note: All seven canonical HTTP(S) citations resolve to accessible pages on the cited organizations' sites.

figures_match_sources.note: The AIA adoption figures are directly supported. The other score-driving citations do not establish eight architecture consolidators, a 4.0x actually-paid small-target EV/EBITDA multiple, a 6.5x $10M+ architecture-platform exit multiple, the BIM adoption history, a 28% owner-age share and succession shortage, or the quoted billing-method percentages.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The architecture-specific role rebuild is prompt-authorized and the declared fractions, pi_dist, and pi_moat are bounded with concrete rationales. However, t50_years=7 depends on an uncited BIM adoption trajectory absent from the historical-analog citation, and the role rationales assert high permit-set and phase automation without corresponding auditable evidence. The resulting judgments are not sufficiently grounded for acceptance.

rubric_consistent.note: The record follows the architecture-office hypothesis structure and addresses pricing deflation and licensure. But B and M use unsupported or population-mismatched inputs, and the pricing mix is cited to a page that does not contain the percentages. The ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL flag is acknowledged: nonzero stored-versus-recomputed deltas on A, B, and S remain within tolerance, but provenance failures are unresolved.

cross_checks_honest.note: The terminal and no-proxy discussions answer the rubric directly. The pricing-model cross-check is not evidence-consistent because it repeats the unsupported 93% hourly/fixed-fee framing and asserts a BIM fee-deflation precedent not established by any cited URL. The uplift is labeled theoretical, but it is driven by uncited high task-automation assumptions.

source_audits.note: The page is an index of weekly A/E M&A updates and demonstrates active sector deal flow. It does not contain the quoted 476 US AEC deals, 42% PE-backed share, named 2024 platform deal counts, or establish eight distinct PE-backed platforms acquiring NAICS 541310 architecture add-ons during the exact 24-month window.

Zweig reports that value/EBITDA rose from 4.23 to 4.28 for its surveyed AEC valuation sample, whose median firm had 45 FTE and about $1.36M EBITDA. It does not report actually paid small-architecture transaction EV/EBITDA, the claimed 2-3x and 3.5-5.5x ranges, or show the market-rate owner-compensation normalization required for a 4.0x buy input.

The March 2025 AIA article reports exactly that 8% of architecture-firm leaders had integrated AI, another 20% were implementing it, and 6% of individual respondents used it regularly. It describes a statistically valid survey of architectural professionals and supports the 0.08 firm-level baseline.

ROG reports 257 mixed architecture, engineering, and environmental transactions: 5.38x median and 7.32x upper quartile for all post-IIJA firms, and 5.52x median, 6.50x mean, and 7.50x upper quartile for engineering-only post-IIJA firms. It does not isolate architecture, $10M+ EBITDA platforms, or observed platform exits supporting 6.5x for NAICS 541310.

The ASCE article reports a current global Bluebeam survey in which 27% of 1,000 AEC professionals used AI. It contains no BIM adoption data, no 43% in 2005 or 96% in 2016 figures, no 2008-2010 50% milestone, and no CAD diffusion timeline.

NCARB reports that nearly 13% of all licensed architects are over 65 and that the architect population declined in 2024. It does not measure firm owners, provide the 60-64 cohort needed to derive 28% age 60+, report an average AIA member age of 54, or document the quoted shortage of qualified and interested successors.

The AIA article qualitatively discusses cost-of-work percentage compensation and B101-2017 fee language. It does not contain the quoted 93% hourly, 92% stipulated-sum, 42% regular hourly, 50% occasional hourly, 70% regular stipulated-sum, or 28% percentage-of-construction-cost survey figures.

reasons: At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://www.asce.org/publications-and-news/civil-engineering-source/article/2025/12/18/architecture-engineering-construction-sector-slow-to-adapt-ai-survey-shows), the cited page contains no BIM or CAD adoption history, so it cannot support the trajectory used to derive inputs.t50_years. Re-run with dated primary BIM/CAD adoption observations matching architecture firms and a transparent 50% crossing calculation.

At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://www.morrisseygoodale.com/resources/ma-update/), the M&A index does not support eight PE-backed architecture consolidators or the quoted deal statistics. Provide a platform-by-platform list with architecture-specific add-ons inside the exact 24-month window and evidence of PE backing.

At inputs.buy_mult.url (https://zweiggroup.com/blogs/the-zweig-letter/zweig-group-releases-2025-valuation-report-of-aec-firms-highlighting-industry-growth-and-resilience), the source reports a mixed-AEC valuation ratio, not actually paid small-architecture EV/EBITDA, and does not show owner-compensation normalization. Use target-size architecture transaction evidence or a complete source-supported EBITDA normalization before setting the buy input.

At inputs.exit_mult.url (https://www.rog-partners.com/inside-the-latest-a/e-business-valuation-and-m-a-transaction-study), the table mixes architecture, engineering, and environmental firms and separately reports engineering-only results, without isolating $10M+ EBITDA architecture-platform exits. Supply recent matching-size architecture transactions or credible architecture platform comps.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://www.ncarb.org/blog/new-data-architect-population-nbtn-2025), 13% of all licensed architects being over 65 does not substantiate 28% of firm owners being 60+ or the succession bonus. Add owner-level age evidence and a separately auditable source documenting the claimed successor shortage.

At inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://www.aia.org/resource-center/calculating-architects-fee-there-better-way), none of the quoted billing-method percentages appears on the page. Cite the actual AIA Firm Survey table or another accessible source reporting the architecture-firm billing mix, then align the pricing-deflation cross-check to those verified percentages.

At inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role, the high construction-document, drafter, visualization, and admin automation fractions are presented as current capability judgments but have no auditable supporting URLs, while the cited AIA adoption article says current use is concentrated in low-invasive tasks and tools still have far to go on inefficient technical tasks. Re-run with concrete current-capability evidence for permit-set/BIM automation and calibrate each fraction to what current AI can substantially do.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, S
